title: Ochnella humilis
taxonomy: Ochnella humilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. This family is a part of the order Malpighiales, which is a large group of flowering plants. The order Malpighiales is in the clade Saxifragidae and the subclass Rosidae. The clade Saxifragidae is in the larger clade rosids and the clade eudicots. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic relationships.
benefits: Ochnella humilis is a plant species in the family Ochnaceae, but there is limited information available on its health benefits. However, some plant species within the same family have been studied for their potential medicinal properties, such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. More research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Ochnella humilis. constituents: Ochnella humilis is a plant species known for its medicinal properties, although information on its active constituents is limited.
